sections.summary: Document title: Standard for Sorghum Grains.
Document code: CXS 172-1989.

sections.section: Adopted in 1989. Revised in 1995. Amended in 2019, 2023.
sections.summary: Adoption history: adopted 1989, revised 1995, amended 2019 and 2023.

sections.section: 2023 Amendments Following  decisions taken  at the  Forty-sixth  Session  of  the  Codex  Alimentarius  Commission  in  December  2023,  amendments  were made in the Annex.
sections.summary: Notice that amendments were made in the Annex following decisions taken at the Forty-sixth Session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023.

sections.section: 2023 editorial correction An editorial correction was made to Section 3.2.3 to read Crotalaria ( Crotalaria spp.) instead of Crotolaria (Crotalaria spp.) and footnote 1 was added in the Annex.
sections.summary: Editorial correction to Section 3.2.3: the species name was corrected to Crotalaria (Crotalaria spp.) and footnote 1 was added in the Annex.

This standard applies to sorghum grains (as defined in Section 2) intended for human consumption, presented packaged or sold loose to the consumer, and excludes other products derived from sorghum grains.

Defines sorghum grains as whole or decorticated grains from Sorghum bicolor (may be dried) and specifies 'whole' as grains after complete threshing without further treatment and 'decorticated' as grains with external casings and whole or parts of the germ removed by mechanical treatment.
Defines sorghum grains as whole or decorticated products of Sorghum bicolor (may be dried) and provides definitions for whole grains and for decorticated grains (mechanical removal of casings/germ).
Defines whole sorghum grains as grains obtained after complete threshing without any further treatment.
Defines decorticated sorghum grains as grains from which external casings and whole or parts of the germ have been removed by appropriate mechanical treatment.

Sets general quality obligations (sorghum must be safe, suitable for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours/odours/living insects and from filth that may hazard health) and specific limits including moisture ≤14.5% m/m, total defects ≤8%, extraneous matter ≤2% (inorganic ≤0.5%), filth ≤0.1%, prohibition of specified toxic/noxious seeds, and tannin limits (whole ≤0.5% d.m., decorticated ≤0.3% d.m.).

Requires sorghum grains to be safe and suitable for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours/odours and living insects, and free from filth (animal-origin impurities, including dead insects) in amounts that may represent a hazard to health.

sections.section: 3.1.1 Sorghum grains shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.
sections.summary: Requires that sorghum grains be safe and suitable for human consumption.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Sorghum grains shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours, and living insects.
sections.summary: Requires that sorghum grains be free from abnormal flavours, odours, and living insects.

sections.section: 3.1.3 Sorghum grains shall be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.
sections.summary: Requires that sorghum grains be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health.

Specifies specific quality factors: maximum moisture 14.5% m/m (with lower limits advised for some destinations), total defects max 8% with defined limits for extraneous matter and filth, requirement to be free from toxic/noxious seeds, and tannin limits of 0.5% d.m. for whole and 0.3% d.m. for decorticated grains.
Sets a maximum moisture content of 14.5% m/m for sorghum grains and advises that lower moisture limits may be required for certain destinations depending on climate and transport/storage duration.

Defines total defects limit at not more than 8% (including extraneous matter, inorganic extraneous matter, filth, blemished/diseased/broken/other grains), limits extraneous matter to ≤2% with inorganic extraneous matter ≤0.5%, and defines filth as animal-origin impurities including dead insects (0.1% m/m max).

sections.section: 3.2.2.2 Filth is impurities of animal origin including dead insects  (0.1 percent m/m max).
sections.summary: Defines filth as impurities of animal origin, including dead insects, and sets a maximum of 0.1% m/m.
Requires products to be free from toxic or noxious seeds in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health and lists examples of such seeds.

sections.section: 3.2.3- Crotalaria ( Crotalaria spp.),  corn  cockle  ( Agrostemma githago L.),  castor  bean  ( Ricinus communis L.), Jimson weed ( Datura spp.), and other seeds that are commonly recognized as harmful to health.
sections.summary: Lists specific toxic or noxious seeds that must be absent, including Crotalaria spp., corn cockle (Agrostemma githago), castor bean (Ricinus communis), Jimson weed (Datura spp.), and other commonly recognized harmful seeds.
Sets tannin-content limits for sorghum: for whole grains ≤0.5% on a dry matter basis and for decorticated grains ≤0.3% on a dry matter basis.

sections.section: 3.2.4(a) For whole sorghum grains, the tannin content shall not exceed 0.5 percent on a dry matter basis.
sections.summary: Limits tannin content of whole sorghum grains to a maximum of 0.5% on a dry matter basis.

sections.section: 3.2.4(b) For decorticated sorghum grains, the tannin content  shall  not exceed 0.3 percent on a dry matter basis.
sections.summary: Sets a maximum tannin limit for decorticated sorghum grains: tannin shall not exceed 0.3% on a dry matter basis.

Overview of contaminant requirements for sorghum: heavy metals must not occur at levels hazardous to health, pesticide residues must comply with Codex MRLs for the commodity, and mycotoxins must comply with Codex mycotoxin limits.
Requires sorghum grains to be free from heavy metals in amounts that may represent a hazard to human health.
Requires sorghum grains to comply with maximum pesticide residue limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission for this commodity.
Requires sorghum grains to comply with maximum mycotoxin limits established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission for this commodity.

Recommends preparing and handling sorghum per the General Principles of Food Hygiene and relevant CAC codes; product should be free from objectionable matter under good manufacturing practice and, when tested, free from microorganisms, parasites, and microbial-origin substances in amounts that may pose a health hazard.

Requires packaging that protects sorghum's hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities; packaging materials must be safe and not impart toxic substances or off-odours/flavours, and sacks must be clean, sturdy and securely closed.
Adds to general labelling rules by requiring the product name on the label to be 'sorghum grains' in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods.

Specifies that methods of analysis and sampling for checking compliance with this standard are those in the Codex Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the provisions.

Annex provides recommended factor descriptions, quality limits and analysis methods for sorghum (users should specify which limit/method when multiple options are given), lists acceptable colours, and sets compositional limits such as decorticated ash Max 1.5% DM, protein Min 7.0% DM, fat Max 4.0% DM, defines defects and visual examination criteria and gives example maximum defect limits (e.g., Max: 5.0%; Max: 1.0%).
